steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the total signatures collected: 40 on Monday plus $s$ on Tuesday, giving a total of $40 + s$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Apply the requirement: the student needs at least 210 signatures to run. "At least" means $\ge$, so: $40 + s \ge 210$, which is the same as $s + 40 \ge 210$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices:
  • Choice A — $s + 40 \le 210$ — says the total is at most 210, meaning the student could stop at 209 signatures and still satisfy the inequality. But 209 is not enough to run. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — $s + 40 \ge 210$ — says the total is at least 210, guaranteeing the student has enough signatures. That is the correct answer.
  • Choice C — $s - 40 \le 210$ — subtracts Monday's signatures instead of adding them. This does not represent the total collected. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $s - 40 \ge 210$ — subtracts Monday's signatures and goes in the right direction, but the structure is wrong; it would require Tuesday alone to be at least 250. Eliminated.
